title.en: Warden of the Celestial Capital on Kunlun

summary.zh: 昆仑之丘的司神，虎身九尾，人面虎爪，替天帝掌管天之九部与苑囿的时节。
summary.en: Tutelary god of the Mound of Kunlun: tiger-bodied with nine tails, a human face and tiger claws, keeper of heaven's nine regions and the seasons of the God's park.

description.zh:
昆仑之丘“是实惟帝之下都”，天帝设在下界的都城，由神陆吾看守。《西次三经》写他“其神状虎身而九尾，人面而虎爪；是神也，司天之九部及帝之囿时”——虎的身体拖着九条尾巴，人的面孔配一双虎爪，职责是掌管天之九域的部界与天帝苑囿的时节。

郭璞注说陆吾“即肩吾也”。《庄子·大宗师》历数得道者，有“肩吾得之，以处大山”一句——庄子笔下坐镇大山的神，与守昆仑的陆吾正相对应。《海内西经》另记昆仑有开明兽，“身大类虎而九首，皆人面，东向立昆仑上”，把守昆仑的九门；袁珂《山海经校注》认为开明兽与陆吾实为同一守护神在不同篇章中的分化。

作为天帝都城的守门神，陆吾与同住昆仑的食人兽土蝼、剧毒的怪鸟钦原比邻，神境昆仑森严可怖的一面，大半由他们三个撑起。
description.en:
The Mound of Kunlun 'is in truth the lower capital of the High God', and the god Luwu keeps it. The 'Classic of the Western Mountains' describes him with a tiger's body and nine tails, a human face and tiger's claws, charged with the nine regions of heaven and the seasons of the God's own park.

Guo Pu's commentary identifies Luwu with Jianwu: the Zhuangzi, listing beings who attained the Way, says 'Jianwu attained it, and thereby dwells on the great mountain' — a fitting match for the warden of Kunlun. The 'Classic of Regions Within the Seas: West' records another guardian, the Kaiming beast, 'large as a tiger, with nine heads, every one a human face', standing on Kunlun facing east to keep its nine gates; Yuan Ke's modern critical edition treats Kaiming and Luwu as two refractions of a single guardian deity.

As gatekeeper of the celestial capital, Luwu shares the mountain with the man-eating Tulou and the lethally venomous bird Qinyuan; among them they account for most of Kunlun's forbidding aspect.

value.en: Tiger's body with nine tails, human face, tiger's claws

value.zh: 司天之九部及帝之囿时
value.en: Keeper of heaven's nine regions and the seasons of the God's park
Jianwu of the Zhuangzi, per Guo Pu

sources.en: Shanhaijing, 'Classic of Regions Within the Seas: West'
Yuan Ke, Shanhaijing Jiaozhu (critical edition with commentary)
